your_book:
Friday was a split-tape day for Jaleel's book, with the overall portfolio barely moving — unrealized gain of just $1.72 (0.01%) on a ~$21,600 account. The winners were narrow but meaningful: NVIDIA surged 4.03%, adding roughly $54 to the position and extending its open gain to +15.0%; Danaher tacked on 1.57% (+$21, now +10.8%); and Lululemon bounced 2.36% despite carrying a -6.7% cost-basis loss and a live guidance-cut flag. On the other side, GILD was the ugliest print — down 3.72% on the day, the kind of move that demands a reason when combined with its drug-pricing policy overhang. NOW slipped 1.04%, RKLB dropped another 1.83% (deepening its -17.8% hole), and VRTX fell 2.24% despite still sitting on a +9.3% open gain.

The drift that deserves honest attention is the ADBE–BSX–LULU cluster. All three carry STRONG BUY or BUY verdicts, yet all three are underwater on cost basis (-8.8%, -6.5%, -6.7% respectively), and all three have active fundamental risk flags — AI-disruption narrative for ADBE, a guidance slash for BSX, and a fifth consecutive negative Americas comp for LULU. Price and thesis are pulling in opposite directions; the model likes the quality, but the market is disagreeing persistently, not just temporarily. RKLB is the starkest case: -17.8% in the hole with an AVOID verdict and a QARP of 39.6 — that's not drift, that's misalignment.

Coming up: ABT reports July 16 — the mild EPS-guide cut flag is live and a miss could dent the book's second-largest healthcare position ($2,724, 12.6% weight). NOW reports July 22; at 14.0% weight and high-multiple exposure, any hawkish guidance language will sting. Watch GILD's unusual Friday drop for any news catalyst over the weekend before Monday's open.

attention.headline: Down 3.72% Friday with no headline — needs a cause
attention.note: A move of that size on a quiet session, layered on top of the MFN drug-pricing overhang, requires Jaleel to confirm whether a catalyst exists before Monday's open; if it's news-driven, the HOLD-QUAL verdict and thin 1.9% weight may still warrant a trim decision.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated, -17.8% loss, fell another 1.83% Friday
attention.note: With a QARP of 39.6 and an AVOID verdict, the position has no fundamental defense — Jaleel should decide whether to exit or formally document why he's holding against the model's signal.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, -6.5% in the hole, earnings July 29
attention.note: Management guiding flat through Q3 while the model rates it STRONG BUY creates a live thesis-vs-price conflict; with earnings three weeks away, Jaleel needs to decide whether to add conviction or reduce ahead of another potential reset.

attention.headline: Earnings July 16 — EPS guide already trimmed
attention.note: At 12.6% of the book ($2,724), ABT is the third-largest position and reports in four days under a mild-but-real EPS flag; a second guidance cut would hurt both the P&L and the defensive anchor role this name plays in the portfolio.
